data.text: I’ll use the `frontend-design` skill for the Svelte UI work, then ground the implementation in the existing routes and e2e test contract before editing.

The worktree already has unrelated dirty skill-link changes, so I’ll keep my staging scoped to the plugin UI and screenshot artifacts only. Next I’m reading the plugin routes, GraphQL surface, and the named e2e spec to pin down the expected selectors and data shapes.
The plugin route itself does not exist yet, but the GraphQL API and e2e contract do. I’m checking nearby project routes now so the new pages fit the existing data-loading style and navigation shell.

The acceptance hinges on accessible roles: registry entries must be `article`s, install scope must be a `radiogroup`, and detail sections must be labelled `region`s. I’m going to add two route pages plus a small nav entry, using the existing FEAT-008 GraphQL queries and the existing ConfirmDialog component.

The initial route implementation is in place. I’m running the frontend formatter and type checks now, since Svelte’s generated route types and dialog bindings tend to catch integration mistakes early.

`bun run format` failed because the frontend dependencies are not installed in this worktree. I’m checking the local package state, then I’ll install in the frontend workspace if needed before rerunning verification.

Prettier rewrote a broad set of pre-existing frontend files. Those were clean before this task, so I’m backing out only that accidental formatting churn and keeping the plugin route/nav/screenshot changes scoped.
Type checking is green; the remaining Svelte warnings are pre-existing files outside this bead. I’m running the named plugin e2e spec next, then I’ll update the new screenshot baselines.

The first e2e run found selector ambiguity, not missing behavior: status/version labels duplicated “installed”, and the dialog close icon included the action label. I’m tightening the accessible names so the test can target the intended badge and confirm buttons unambiguously.

The second e2e run hit a port collision: another execution worktree has a Playwright preview server bound to `4173`, so my run connected to the wrong app and got 404s. I’m waiting for that server to clear rather than killing an unrelated process, then I’ll rerun the plugin spec against this worktree.

Three of four plugin tests are now passing; the remaining failure is another accessible text collision between the update badge and the update button. I switched the update action to an icon button with an `aria-label`, so the badge is the only visible “update” text while the button remains discoverable by role.

The plugin e2e contract is now green: 4/4 passed. I’m updating the screenshot baselines next; adding the Plugins nav item changes the shared shell, so the screenshot run may refresh existing shell snapshots as well as add the new plugin list/detail baselines.

Port `4173` is occupied again by a preview server from a different execution worktree, so I’m pausing for it to exit before running screenshot updates. This avoids accidentally writing screenshots from the wrong app.

The conflicting server is long-lived, so I’m switching verification to a temporary Playwright config on port `4183`. That keeps the committed project config unchanged and lets the screenshot updater talk to this worktree explicitly.

Screenshot baselines are written and the screenshot spec diff is now scoped to just the two plugin entries. I’m rerunning type checks and then I’ll do a final screenshot verification pass on the alternate port.

Verification is complete: `plugins.spec.ts` passed 4/4, `svelte-check` passed with only pre-existing warnings, and the screenshot suite passed 18/18 on the alternate port. I’m staging only the plugin UI, shared nav/dialog tweak, screenshot spec, and generated PNG baselines; the pre-existing dirty skill symlinks stay untouched.

Implemented and committed `ddx-4cecb7d5`.

Commit: `9f0fae69 feat: add plugin registry UI [ddx-4cecb7d5]`

Verified:
- `bun run check` passed
- `bun x playwright test e2e/plugins.spec.ts` passed 4/4
- `e2e/screenshots.spec.ts` passed 18/18 using a temporary port due a `4173` port collision

Unrelated pre-existing skill-link dirt remains unstaged.
